1	package bead
2	
3	import (
4		"testing"
5		"time"
6	
7		"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
8	)
9	
10	// helpers shared across queue lifecycle tests
11	
12	func createBeadWithQueueRank(t *testing.T, s *Store, rank int) *Bead {
13		t.Helper()
14		b := &Bead{Title: "queue-rank test bead"}
15		require.NoError(t, s.Create(b))
16		require.NoError(t, s.Update(b.ID, func(bead *Bead) {
17			if bead.Extra == nil {
18				bead.Extra = make(map[string]any)
19			}
20			bead.Extra[queueRankKey] = rank
21		}))
22		got, err := s.Get(b.ID)
23		require.NoError(t, err)
24		return got
25	}
26	
27	func assertQueueRank(t *testing.T, s *Store, id string, want int) {
28		t.Helper()
29		got, err := s.Get(id)
30		require.NoError(t, err)
31		rank, ok := parseQueueRank(got.Extra[queueRankKey])
32		if !ok {
33			t.Errorf("parseQueueRank: no queue-rank found on bead %s (Extra=%v)", id, got.Extra)
34			return
35		}
36		if rank != want {
37			t.Errorf("queue-rank on bead %s: got %d, want %d", id, rank, want)
38		}
39	}
40	
41	func assertQueueRankInArchive(t *testing.T, s *Store, id string, want int) {
42		t.Helper()
43		got, err := s.GetWithArchive(id)
44		require.NoError(t, err)
45		rank, ok := parseQueueRank(got.Extra[queueRankKey])
46		if !ok {
47			t.Errorf("parseQueueRank: no queue-rank found in archive for bead %s (Extra=%v)", id, got.Extra)
48			return
49		}
50		if rank != want {
51			t.Errorf("queue-rank in archive for bead %s: got %d, want %d", id, rank, want)
52		}
53	}
54	
55	// TestQueueRank_PreservedAcrossClaim verifies that Claim does not delete queue-rank from Extra.
56	func TestQueueRank_PreservedAcrossClaim(t *testing.T) {
57		s := newTestStore(t)
58		b := createBeadWithQueueRank(t, s, 5)
59		require.NoError(t, s.Claim(b.ID, "worker"))
60		assertQueueRank(t, s, b.ID, 5)
61	}
62	
63	// TestQueueRank_PreservedAcrossUnclaim verifies that Unclaim does not delete queue-rank from Extra.
64	func TestQueueRank_PreservedAcrossUnclaim(t *testing.T) {
65		s := newTestStore(t)
66		b := createBeadWithQueueRank(t, s, 5)
67		require.NoError(t, s.Claim(b.ID, "worker"))
68		require.NoError(t, s.Unclaim(b.ID))
69		assertQueueRank(t, s, b.ID, 5)
70	}
71	
72	// TestQueueRank_PreservedAcrossClose verifies that Close does not delete queue-rank from Extra.
73	func TestQueueRank_PreservedAcrossClose(t *testing.T) {
74		s := newTestStore(t)
75		b := createBeadWithQueueRank(t, s, 5)
76		require.NoError(t, s.Close(b.ID))
77		assertQueueRank(t, s, b.ID, 5)
78	}
79	
80	// TestQueueRank_PreservedAcrossReopen verifies that Reopen does not delete queue-rank from Extra.
81	func TestQueueRank_PreservedAcrossReopen(t *testing.T) {
82		s := newTestStore(t)
83		b := createBeadWithQueueRank(t, s, 5)
84		require.NoError(t, s.Close(b.ID))
85		require.NoError(t, s.Reopen(b.ID, "reopen reason", ""))
86		assertQueueRank(t, s, b.ID, 5)
87	}
88	
89	// TestQueueRank_PreservedAcrossReconcile verifies that ReconcileLifecycleMetadata
90	// does not delete queue-rank from Extra.
91	func TestQueueRank_PreservedAcrossReconcile(t *testing.T) {
92		s := newTestStore(t)
93		b := createBeadWithQueueRank(t, s, 5)
94		_, err := s.ReconcileLifecycleMetadata(ReconcileOptions{Apply: true, Now: time.Now().UTC()})
95		require.NoError(t, err)
96		assertQueueRank(t, s, b.ID, 5)
97	}
98	
99	// TestQueueRank_PreservedAcrossMigrateLifecycle verifies that MigrateLifecycle
100	// does not delete queue-rank from Extra.
101	func TestQueueRank_PreservedAcrossMigrateLifecycle(t *testing.T) {
102		s := newTestStore(t)
103		b := createBeadWithQueueRank(t, s, 5)
104		_, err := s.MigrateLifecycle()
105		require.NoError(t, err)
106		assertQueueRank(t, s, b.ID, 5)
107	}
108	
109	// TestQueueRank_PreservedAcrossArchive verifies that ArchiveWithEvents does not
110	// delete queue-rank; the archived bead must carry the same rank.
111	func TestQueueRank_PreservedAcrossArchive(t *testing.T) {
112		s := newTestStore(t)
113		b := createBeadWithQueueRank(t, s, 5)
114		require.NoError(t, s.Close(b.ID))
115	
116		policy := migratePolicy() // statuses=[closed], MinAge=0, MinActiveCount=0
117		_, err := s.ArchiveWithEvents(policy)
118		require.NoError(t, err)
119	
120		assertQueueRankInArchive(t, s, b.ID, 5)
121	}
122	
123	// TestQueueRank_RoundTripViaJSONL verifies that queue-rank written as an int
124	// survives a JSONL write+read round-trip and parseQueueRank succeeds.
125	func TestQueueRank_RoundTripViaJSONL(t *testing.T) {
126		s := newTestStore(t)
127		b := createBeadWithQueueRank(t, s, 7)
128		// Read back from disk (forces JSONL decode; int 7 will come back as float64(7)).
129		got, err := s.Get(b.ID)
130		require.NoError(t, err)
131		rank, ok := parseQueueRank(got.Extra[queueRankKey])
132		if !ok {
133			t.Fatalf("parseQueueRank after JSONL round-trip: not ok (Extra=%v)", got.Extra)
134		}
135		if rank != 7 {
136			t.Errorf("queue-rank after JSONL round-trip: got %d, want 7", rank)
137		}
138	}
139	
140	// TestQueueRank_SetFlagStringRoundTrip simulates `ddx bead update --set
141	// queue-rank=5` which stores the value as a string. The string must survive
142	// JSONL round-trip and parseQueueRank must return the numeric value.
143	func TestQueueRank_SetFlagStringRoundTrip(t *testing.T) {
144		s := newTestStore(t)
145		b := &Bead{Title: "string queue-rank test"}
146		require.NoError(t, s.Create(b))
147		require.NoError(t, s.Update(b.ID, func(bead *Bead) {
148			if bead.Extra == nil {
149				bead.Extra = make(map[string]any)
150			}
151			bead.Extra[queueRankKey] = "5" // string, as produced by --set flag
152		}))
153		got, err := s.Get(b.ID)
154		require.NoError(t, err)
155		rank, ok := parseQueueRank(got.Extra[queueRankKey])
156		if !ok {
157			t.Fatalf("parseQueueRank with string input: not ok (Extra=%v)", got.Extra)
158		}
159		if rank != 5 {
160			t.Errorf("parseQueueRank with string input: got %d, want 5", rank)
161		}
162	}

data.output:
commit 840bd13f13a5054ca2534ed020ca4688beca3ec5
Author: Erik LaBianca <erik.labianca@gmail.com>
Date:   Sun May 10 08:13:32 2026 -0400

    fix(agent/land): SyncWorkTreeToHead unconditionally skips beads JSONL files
    
    .ddx/beads.jsonl and .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl are live-state files
    governed by bead.Store WithLock. The committed worktree snapshot is
    stale (agent claim-time state); the main worktree copy is always
    authoritative. Add alwaysSkipSyncPaths so SyncWorkTreeToHead never
    overwrites them regardless of dirty-before-land state.
    
    Add regression tests TestSyncWorkTreeToHead_DoesNotClobberBeadsJSONL
    and TestSyncWorkTreeToHead_DoesNotClobberBeadsArchiveJSONL.
    
    Add TestQueueRank_* lifecycle tests verifying queue-rank is preserved
    across Claim, Unclaim, Close, Reopen, ReconcileLifecycleMetadata,
    MigrateLifecycle, ArchiveWithEvents, and JSONL round-trip (both int
    and string forms). [ddx-2257460f]

commit 81faaa43e4a5695362566b9be051165933c6a835
Author: Erik LaBianca <erik.labianca@gmail.com>
Date:   Sun May 10 08:15:17 2026 -0400

    fix(agent/land): SyncWorkTreeToHead unconditionally skips beads.jsonl
    
    Before this fix, the post-land syncWorkTreeToHeadExcludingPaths step would
    restore .ddx/beads.jsonl and .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl from the version
    committed in the landed worktree — a stale snapshot from claim time. Any
    operator writes (e.g. `ddx bead update --set queue-rank=N`) that arrived
    after the agent's claim commit were silently reverted on the next land.
    
    Fix: RealLandingGitOps.SyncWorkTreeToHead now passes alwaysSkipSyncPaths
    (containing both JSONL files) to syncWorkTreeToHeadExcludingPaths so they
    are never restored from a landed commit, regardless of dirty-before-land
    state. Both files are governed by bead.Store WithLock and their live state
    is always authoritative.
    
    Adds regression tests in execute_bead_land_test.go (AC2+3) and a full
    queue-rank lifecycle preservation suite in queue_lifecycle_test.go (AC4-12). [ddx-2257460f]

1737	// TestSyncWorkTreeToHead_DoesNotClobberBeadsJSONL verifies that
1738	// RealLandingGitOps.SyncWorkTreeToHead never overwrites .ddx/beads.jsonl in
1739	// the main worktree, even when the landed commit modified it.
1740	func TestSyncWorkTreeToHead_DoesNotClobberBeadsJSONL(t *testing.T) {
1741		r := newLandTestRepo(t)
1742	
1743		// Base commit includes .ddx/beads.jsonl with open state.
1744		r.writeFile(".ddx/beads.jsonl", `{"id":"ddx-test","status":"open"}`+"\n")
1745		r.runGit("add", ".ddx/beads.jsonl")
1746		r.runGit("commit", "-m", "add beads.jsonl")
1747		fromRev := r.resolveRef("refs/heads/main")
1748	
1749		// Landing commit: agent snapshot has in_progress, no queue-rank.
1750		r.writeFile(".ddx/beads.jsonl", `{"id":"ddx-test","status":"in_progress"}`+"\n")
1751		r.writeFile("feature.txt", "feature\n")
1752		r.runGit("add", ".ddx/beads.jsonl", "feature.txt")
1753		r.runGit("commit", "-m", "feat: add feature [ddx-test]")
1754	
1755		// Operator's live state written after claim: queue-rank preserved.
1756		liveContent := `{"id":"ddx-test","status":"open","extra":{"queue-rank":5}}` + "\n"
1757		r.writeFile(".ddx/beads.jsonl", liveContent)
1758	
1759		ops := RealLandingGitOps{}
1760		if err := ops.SyncWorkTreeToHead(r.dir, fromRev); err != nil {
1761			t.Fatalf("SyncWorkTreeToHead: %v", err)
1762		}
1763	
1764		got, err := os.ReadFile(filepath.Join(r.dir, ".ddx", "beads.jsonl"))
1765		if err != nil {
1766			t.Fatalf("reading beads.jsonl: %v", err)
1767		}
1768		if string(got) != liveContent {
1769			t.Errorf("beads.jsonl was clobbered by SyncWorkTreeToHead\ngot:  %q\nwant: %q", string(got), liveContent)
1770		}
1771	}
1772

1789	
1790		// Live archive has additional data the operator added after the claim.

1794
